title: scrophularia alata subsp. umbrosa
intro: Scrophularia alata subsp. umbrosa, also known as the woodland figwort or umbrosa figwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the figwort family, Scrophulariaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its roots and leaves possessing anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of Scrophularia alata subsp. umbrosa include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine.
